description: Fix three CLI UX issues discovered during fresh-user experience testing: (1) bare `opentabs` command degrades poorly when server is down, (2) `opentabs audit` doesn't hint about --file when server is offline, (3) `opentabs config set browser-tool.` doesn't list available browser tools like `tool.` does. These are MEDIUM-severity issues affecting new-user discoverability and error guidance. Known LOW-severity issues not addressed: `config show` renders `permissions` as raw JSON, doctor hint text doesn't mention OpenCode, `config set tool.` says 'No tools available' when browser tools exist.

userStories.title: Graceful fallback when bare `opentabs` runs with server offline
userStories.description: As a new user, I want `opentabs` (no arguments) to show a helpful welcome/status message instead of an error exit code when the server isn't running, so that my first interaction with the CLI feels welcoming rather than broken.
acceptanceCriteria: When server is running: `opentabs` (no args) shows status output (same as `opentabs status`) and exits 0
When server is NOT running: `opentabs` (no args) shows a friendly message like 'OpenTabs MCP Server is not running. Get started: opentabs start' and exits 0 (not 1)
The bare-command output when offline does NOT show the full help text ŌĆö it shows a concise status + getting-started hint
The bare-command output when offline includes a hint to run `opentabs --help` for available commands
`opentabs status` behavior remains unchanged (exits 1 when server is down, as it's explicitly asking for status)
userStories.notes:
The root command action is in `platform/cli/src/cli.ts` line 35: `.action((_options, command: Command) => handleStatus(command.optsWithGlobals()))`. This calls `handleStatus` from `platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts` which calls `process.exit(1)` when the server is unreachable (line 167). The fix should create a new wrapper function (e.g., `handleBareCommand`) in `cli.ts` that catches the 'server not running' case and shows a friendly message + exits 0 instead. Do NOT change `handleStatus` itself ŌĆö that function should keep its current behavior for the explicit `opentabs status` command. The wrapper can either: (a) try the health check itself and show friendly output on failure, or (b) call `handleStatus` in a try/catch and intercept the exit. Option (a) is cleaner. The wrapper should attempt a health check, and if it succeeds, delegate to `handleStatus`. If it fails (connection refused), print a concise message like:

OpenTabs MCP Server
  Status: not running

Get started:
  opentabs start          Start the MCP server
  opentabs --help         Show all commands

Import `resolvePort` from `../parse-port.js` and `isConnectionRefused` from `../config.js` for the health check.

userStories.title: Hint about `--file` flag when `opentabs audit` fails due to offline server
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs audit` to tell me about the `--file` flag when the server is not running, so that I can read persistent audit logs without needing to discover the flag on my own.
acceptanceCriteria: When server is not running, `opentabs audit` error output includes a hint like: 'Tip: Use --file to read from the persistent disk log'
The hint appears after the existing 'MCP server is not running' / 'Start it with: opentabs start' messages
The hint is styled with `pc.dim()` to be secondary to the main error message
The hint also appears for timeout errors (server not responding)

userStories.title: List available browser tools with `opentabs config set browser-tool.`
userStories.description: As a user, I want `opentabs config set browser-tool.` (with just the prefix) to list available browser tools, matching the existing behavior of `opentabs config set tool.` which lists plugin tools.
acceptanceCriteria: `opentabs config set browser-tool.` (no tool name after the dot) lists all available browser tools by fetching from the server health endpoint
Each browser tool name is printed on its own line, indented, similar to the plugin tool listing
If the server is not running, shows a helpful error message (same pattern as the plugin tool listing: 'Cannot reach the MCP server to list available browser tools')
If no browser tools exist (edge case), shows 'No browser tools available'
The listing includes a usage hint: 'Usage: opentabs config set browser-tool.<name> enabled|disabled'
`opentabs config set browser-tool.browser_execute_script disabled` still works (existing behavior unchanged)
userStories.notes:
Edit `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts`. The fix mirrors the existing `tool.` listing pattern.

1. Add a `fetchBrowserToolNames` function (near line 150, after `fetchToolNames`) that fetches from the health endpoint and returns `cachedBrowserTools` names. The health endpoint response includes a `disabledBrowserTools` array, but that only has disabled ones. Instead, look at the health response ŌĆö it has `browserToolCount` but not the actual names. Actually, check the health endpoint code at `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts` around line 277 ŌĆö the health response includes `cachedBrowserTools` data implicitly via `browserToolCount`. We need the actual names. Looking at the health endpoint code, it doesn't expose all browser tool names. Two options: (a) add a browser tool names field to the health endpoint, or (b) use a different approach. Option (b): the CLI can use existing data ŌĆö all browser tool names start with `browser_` or `extension_`. The health endpoint doesn't list all of them. Best approach: add `browserToolNames` to the health response.

2. In `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts`, around line 303, add `browserToolNames: state.cachedBrowserTools.map(c => c.name)` to the health response object.

3. Back in `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts`, add `handleListBrowserTools` (similar to `handleListTools` at line 168). It should fetch from health, extract `browserToolNames`, and print them.

4. In `handleConfigSet` (line 393), add a check before the `key.startsWith(BROWSER_TOOL_PREFIX)` check (line 407): `if (key === BROWSER_TOOL_PREFIX) return handleListBrowserTools(options);`

5. Update the `BROWSER_TOOL_PREFIX` constant usage so that `browser-tool.` with no suffix triggers the listing.

Files to edit: `platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts` (~lines 150, 393-408) and `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.ts` (~line 303). Also update any related test files if they validate the health endpoint shape: `platform/mcp-server/src/http-routes.test.ts`.
